cases.title: JOIN_ON with scope - no DB-wide columns
cases.sql: SELECT * FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id JOIN products p ON p|

expected.comment: Scope=[users as u, orders as o, products as p]. Only scope columns starting with 'p' in schema order. DB-wide excluded.

cases.title: JOIN_ON generic prefix - only scope table columns
cases.sql: SELECT * FROM orders o JOIN users u ON u|

expected.comment: Scope=[orders as o, users as u]. Alias-exact-match for 'u' in schema order.

cases.title: JOIN_ON with CURRENT_TABLE not in scope - CURRENT_TABLE ignored
cases.sql: SELECT * FROM orders o JOIN products p ON p.id = |

expected.comment: Scope=[orders (alias o), products (alias p)]. CURRENT_TABLE=users not in scope. p.id is on left of =, so filtered out.

cases.title: JOIN_ON with CURRENT_TABLE in scope - CURRENT_TABLE included
cases.sql: SELECT * FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u|

expected.comment: Scope=[users as u, orders as o]. CURRENT_TABLE=users in scope. Alias-exact-match in schema order.
